Lateral Movement. The adversary is trying to move through your AI environment.

Lateral Movement consists of techniques that adversaries may use to gain access to and control other
Search Open Technical Databases. Adversaries may search for publicly available research and technical documentation to learn how and where AI is used within a victim organization.
Verify that sign, range, and input validation techniques are used to prevent integer overflows.

Use Pre-Trained Model. Adversaries may use an off-the-shelf pre-trained model as a proxy for the victim model to aid in staging the attack.
Practical Exercises. Provide practical exercises in literacy training that simulate events and incidents.

Excessive Use of Hard-Coded Literals in Initialization. The product initializes a data element using a hard-coded literal that is not a simple integer or static constant element.
Empty Code Block. The source code contains a block that does not contain any code, i.e., the block is empty.
Source Code File with Excessive Number of Lines of Code. A source code file has too many lines of code.
Verify that user set passwords are at least 8 characters in length although a minimum of 15 characters is strongly recommended.
Verify that users can change their password.
Verify that password change functionality requires the user's current and new password.

Verify that the application sanitizes content before it is sent to memcache to prevent injection attacks.
Verify that WebSocket over TLS (WSS) is used for all WebSocket connections.
Verify that, during the initial HTTP WebSocket handshake, the Origin header field is checked against a list of origins allowed for the application.
Verify that only the latest recommended versions of the TLS protocol are enabled, such as TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3. The latest version of the TLS protocol must be the preferred option.
